top_signals.title: Oil Refiners Are Cashing In on a Market That Won’t Stay Broken
top_signals.summary: Oil refiners have stumbled into one of the best profit environments in years. Crude prices have fallen back to where they traded before the Iran war erupted. But gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel remain stubbornly expensive. That combination has pushed refining margins to extraordinary levels, giving refiners a windfall that few expected just weeks after the Strait of Hormuz reopened. The benchmark U.S. 3-2-1 crack spread—a closely watched measure of refining profitability—recently climbed above $60 per barrel, the highest level on record.…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US launches air strikes as Iran targets tankers with cruise missiles
top_signals.summary: Donald Trump said Washington would hit the Islamic republic ‘hard’ on Monday and Tuesday nights
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Industry bodies call on Burnham to bring down energy bills to fire up growth
top_signals.summary: Andy Burnham’s government must slash green levies on companies’ energy bills and bring forward support for energy-intensive industries if it wants to fire up Britain’s economy and win over a sceptical private sector, two of Britain’s largest industry bodies have said. In a joint report published today, the CBI and Energy UK called on the [...]

top_signals.title: Helsing raises $1.8 billion in Europe’s biggest defense-startup round
top_signals.summary: Defense technology has been one of the fastest-growing areas of venture capital funding on the back of rising military budgets, and in Europe especially.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Burnham urged to go further to fix ‘broken’ business rates
top_signals.summary: Incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham must broaden his plans to reform the “broken and unsustainable” business rates system, hospitality and retail industry leaders have warned. The former mayor of Greater Manchester has already pledged to slash business rates for high street firms but a coalition of trade bodies has urged him to go further. Ros [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Heatwave slows retail sales but World Cup boosts online shopping
top_signals.summary: June’s scorching heat slowed retail sales, but Brits opted to shop from home instead, sending online sales soaring.  Total retail sales in the UK slowed from 3.7 per cent in May to 1.9 per cent in June year on year, in line with this year’s average growth rate, according to the British Retail Consortium (BRC). [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Top investors managing $3tn to gain access to UK infrastructure projects via AI platform
top_signals.summary: Top global investors managing around $3 trillion in assets will be given access to “fragmented” British infrastructure projects under plans for an AI platform designed to fix the industry’s investment woes. Both national and regional projects will be connected to trillions of global capital through the AI platform, dubbed InvestConnect, in a bid to accelerate [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Surviving the Uchiwa Festival, Japan’s hottest ‘matsuri’
top_signals.summary: Temperatures in Kumagaya, home of the raucous Uchiwa Festival, often top 40 degrees Celsius.
